example_label: Solve the quadratic equation 2x² + 7x + 3 = using the quadratic formule

range_hints.a: Must be non-zero (otherwise not quadratic); typical: -100 to 100

result_context: The solutions x1 ({x1}) and x2 ({x2}) are the roots where the parabola crosses the x-axis; discriminante ({discriminante}) determines solution type (positive=real, zero=real, negative=complex).
mistakes: Not checking if a=0 (would make it linear, not quadratic)
Forgetting the ± gives two solutions, not just one
Misapplying signs when b is negative in the formule
